attribution: Sayings gathered from public-domain translations and traditional collections, with brief attributed quotations of the modern saints; wording follows common English renderings.

quotes.q: I saw the snares that the enemy spreads out over the world, and I said groaning: what can get through such snares? Then I heard a voice saying to me: humility.
Our life and our death is with our neighbour. If we gain our brother, we have gained God.
Without temptations no one can be saved.
A time is coming when men will go mad, and when they see someone who is not mad, they will attack him, saying: you are mad, you are not like us.
The truly intelligent man pursues one sole objective: to obey and to conform to the God of all.
I have often repented of having spoken, but never of having remained silent.
Flee, be silent, pray always, for these are the sources of sinlessness.
If we keep remembering the wrongs which men have done us, we destroy the power of the remembrance of God.
A man may seem to be silent, but if his heart is condemning others, he is babbling ceaselessly.
Teach your mouth to say that which you have in your heart.
Do not give your heart to that which does not satisfy your heart.
If man remembered that it is written, 'By your words you will be justified and by your words you will be condemned,' he would choose to remain silent.
Go, sit in your cell, and your cell will teach you everything.
If a man's deeds are not in harmony with his prayer, he labours in vain.
Unless he keeps the commandments of God, a man cannot make progress, not even in a single virtue.
Believe me, I do not know whether I have even begun to repent.
quotes.by: Abba Sisoes, at the end of his life

quotes.q: In the beginning there are a great many battles and a good deal of suffering for those who are advancing towards God; and afterwards, ineffable joy.
We have put aside the easy burden, which is self-accusation, and weighed ourselves down with the heavy one, self-justification.
Of all evil suggestions, the most terrible is the prompting to follow your own heart.
The closer we come to God, the closer we come to our neighbour; and the closer we come to our neighbour, the closer we come to God.
quotes.src: adapted from his image of the circle

quotes.q: He who finds fault with himself accepts all things cheerfully — misfortune, loss, disgrace, dishonour, and every other kind of adversity.
When you remember God, multiply your prayer, so that when you forget Him, the Lord may remind you.
For the Son of God became man so that we might become god.
The holy and inspired Scriptures are fully sufficient for the proclamation of the truth.
The bread which you hold back belongs to the hungry; the coat you keep put away belongs to the naked.
A tree is known by its fruit; a man by his deeds. A good deed is never lost.
He who sows courtesy reaps friendship, and he who plants kindness gathers love.
When you sit down to eat, pray. When you eat bread, give thanks to Him who is so generous to you.
What has not been assumed has not been healed; but what is united to God is saved.
We ought to remember God even more often than we draw breath.
Give something, however small, to the one in need. For it is not small to one who has nothing; neither is it small to God, if we have given what we could.
Prayer is the place of refuge for every worry, a foundation for cheerfulness, a source of constant happiness, a protection against sadness.
It is impossible for one who prays with zeal to go on sinning.
Feeding the hungry is a greater work than raising the dead.
The Church is a hospital, and not a courtroom, for souls.
If you cannot find Christ in the beggar at the church door, you will not find Him in the chalice.
Let no one weep for his iniquities, for pardon has shone forth from the grave.
No matter how just your words may be, you ruin everything when you speak with anger.
Glory to God for all things.
quotes.by: St John Chrysostom, his last words

quotes.q: O Lord and Master of my life, take from me the spirit of sloth, despair, lust of power, and idle talk; but give rather the spirit of chastity, humility, patience, and love to Thy servant.
Virtues are formed by prayer. Prayer preserves temperance. Prayer suppresses anger.
The glory of God is a living man; and the life of man consists in beholding God.
It is better for me to die in Christ Jesus than to reign over the ends of the earth.
Where the bishop is, there let the people be; even as where Jesus Christ is, there is the catholic Church.
Eighty-six years have I served Him, and He never did me any wrong: how then can I blaspheme my King and my Saviour?
He can no longer have God for his Father, who has not the Church for his mother.
quotes.src: On the Unity of the Church

quotes.q: Let us fix our eyes on the blood of Christ, and understand how precious it is to His Father.
You can kill us, but you cannot hurt us.
The dragon sits by the side of the road, watching those who pass. Beware lest he devour you. We go to the Father of souls, but it is necessary to pass by the dragon.
Thou hast made us for Thyself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it rests in Thee.
No one heals himself by wounding another.
The blood of the martyrs is the seed of the Church.
Prayer is conversation with God.
Angels are a light for monks, and the monastic life is a light for all men.
Do not be surprised that you fall every day; do not give up, but stand your ground courageously.
Prayer is by nature a dialogue and a union of man with God.
Humility is the one virtue that no devil can imitate.
This life has been given to you for repentance; do not waste it in vain pursuits.
As a handful of sand thrown into the ocean, so are the sins of all flesh compared with the mind of God.
Be at peace with your own soul; then heaven and earth will be at peace with you.
The ladder of the Kingdom is within you, hidden in your soul.
Love sinners, but hate their deeds.
What is a merciful heart? It is a heart on fire for the whole of creation — for men, for the birds, for the beasts, for the demons, and for all that exists.
Silence is the mystery of the age to come, but words are instruments of this world.
Love is a holy state of the soul, disposing it to value knowledge of God above all created things.
The one who loves God cannot help loving every man as himself.
Blessed is the man who can love all men equally.
Prayer is the ascent of the mind to God.
Prayer is not perfect when the monk is conscious of himself and of the fact that he is praying.
It is a greater miracle to be patient and refrain from anger than it is to command the demons which fly through the air.
I do not worship matter; I worship the Creator of matter, who became matter for my sake.
The whole earth is a living icon of the face of God.
God is fire that warms and kindles the heart. If we feel in our hearts the cold which comes from the devil, let us call upon the Lord, and He will come to warm our hearts.
Acquire a peaceful spirit, and around you thousands will be saved.
The true aim of our Christian life consists in the acquisition of the Holy Spirit of God.
You cannot be too gentle, too kind. Shun even to appear harsh in your treatment of one another.
My joy! Christ is risen!
quotes.by: St Seraphim of Sarov, his greeting to all

quotes.q: Prayer is the test of everything; prayer is also the source of everything; prayer is the driving force of everything.
Descend with the mind into the heart, and there stand before the face of the Lord, ever-present, all-seeing, within you.
Of all holy works, the education of children is the most holy.
Prayer is the breathing of the soul; prayer is our spiritual food and drink.

Never confuse the person, formed in the image of God, with the evil that is in him; because evil is but a chance misfortune, an illness, a devilish reverie. But the very essence of the person is the image of God.
When you pray, endeavour to pray more for others than for yourself alone.
Read the Gospel with extreme reverence and attention. In it everything is important, everything is full of meaning.
A true Christian is made by faith and love toward Christ.
Where there is simplicity, there are a hundred angels; but where there is cleverness, there is not one.
We must live on earth as a wheel turns: only one point touches the ground, while the rest strains upward.
Know yourself, and that is enough for you.
Do not seek perfection from others when you have not yet attained it yourself.
Never think that anything in your life happens by chance. Everything is sent for the good of your soul.
From this day, from this hour, from this minute, let us strive to love God above all.
The grace of God always assists those who struggle.
quotes.by: St John of Shanghai and San Francisco

quotes.q: Faith without works and works without faith will both alike be condemned, for the doer of good must offer both together.
Blessed is he who has become the close friend of faith and of prayer: he lives in singleness of mind and makes prayer and faith a pair.
Prayer makes the soul a temple of God.
The Holy Spirit is light and life, a living fountain of spiritual gifts.
Do not say that it is impossible to be moved by the Divine Spirit; do not say that without Him it is possible to be saved.
Hold fast to patience, and in all that befalls you, give thanks; for thanksgiving intercedes before God for our weakness.
quotes.by: Ss Barsanuphius and John of Gaza

quotes.q: Humility does not fall, for it lies lower than all; and he who lies on the ground has nowhere to fall.
Every day and every hour we are required to show love — first to God, and then to every man.
Keep thy mind in hell, and despair not.
quotes.src: the word given him by the Lord

quotes.q: Blessed is the soul that loves her brother, for our brother is our life.
To pray for people is to shed blood.
If you want to help the Church, correct yourself, and immediately a small part of the Church is corrected.
The person who has good thoughts sees everything as good.
Prayer from the heart, with pain of heart, is heard at once.
Whoever wants to become a Christian must first become a poet.
Christ is our friend, our brother; He is whatever is beautiful and good.
Love Christ and put nothing before His love.
Prayer is infinite creation, the supreme art.
Stand at the brink of the abyss of despair, and when you see that you cannot bear it any more, draw back a little, and have a cup of tea.
Say the Jesus Prayer — 'Lord Jesus Christ, have mercy on me' — and the grace of God will come.
I came to love suffering, for it wonderfully purifies the soul.
quotes.by: St Luke of Simferopol, the Surgeon

quotes.q: Bless my enemies, O Lord; even I bless them and do not curse them.
Live in such a way that not only God loves you, but people love you too.
quotes.by: St Gabriel of Georgia, Fool for Christ
